title: rosa canina var. subcanina
intro: Rosa canina var. subcanina, also known as the dwarf or alpine dog rose, is a shrub-like perennial plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and carotenoids. Rosa canina var. subcanina has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. subcanina, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, are commonly used to promote wound healing, alleviate gastrointestinal issues, and support immune function.
